behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in message-slicing/order (wrong count, wrong end of list). Required setup: call the prompt/history-preparation function with >10 messages and assert only the latest 10 are forwarded in correct order. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified because the behavior text is a deterministic trimming rule ("trimmed to the most recent 10 messages") and does not require real DB/OS/network or real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a history of 12-15 messages with identifiable IDs/timestamps, run the history-trimming function, and assert output length is 10 and equals the last 10 original messages in order; also assert no trim when <=10.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (relevance/grounding of generated text). Required setup: run real prompt+history through the real LLM and evaluate relevance against question-focused acceptance criteria or rubric across varied prompts. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed model can be made always on-topic, hiding true failures. REJECTION-GATE: System is too low because real HTTP alone does not validate semantic quality; Workflow is disqualified because this behavior is about one generation quality check, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed dataset of customer questions/history pairs, call the production generation path with the real LLM, and score whether responses answer the asked question (not generic/off-topic) using human-labeled rubric or calibrated evaluator; fail if relevance rate drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error in tone classification, plus handoff to regeneration. Required setup: feed candidate responses for casual-tier users through the real tone-check mechanism and verify over-formal drafts are flagged and regeneration path is invoked. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network requirement beyond normal app call, so not forced to System; (b) correctness depends on real model tone judgment, so above Integration; (c) can be validated with one real agentic judgment plus regeneration trigger assertion, so Workflow not required.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a set of intentionally formal candidate drafts for casual-tier customers, run tone-check with real model/classifier, assert they are flagged and regeneration is called; include borderline samples to catch misclassification drift.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between chat orchestration and persistence (missing regeneration entries, wrong timestamp fields/order). Required setup: run service + repository against a real local test DB (or transactional test DB) and verify stored records. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB wiring; (b) correctness does not depend on AI quality; (c) no multi-agent quality pipeline needed.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a chat flow in test mode that includes at least one regeneration event, then query DB and assert all exchange records are persisted (user text, initial draft, regenerated draft, final served response marker) with expected timestamp semantics and ordering.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: orchestration/state handoff error (wrong response selected/returned, stale draft leakage). Required setup: wire controller/service/tone-check decision + response-selection path together, force a regeneration trigger, and assert delivered payload is the regenerated text only. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior is about replacement/visibility control flow ("second response replaces the first") rather than model quality; this can be exposed with deterministic stubs for model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service flow with mocked deterministic first and second drafts and forced tone-check trigger; assert API/output channel contains only second draft, and rejected draft is absent from customer-visible response while still audit-logged as rejected if required.
